title: navarretia androsacea
intro: Navarretia androsacea, also known as Mountain Fiddleneck, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Mexico, belonging to the Phrymaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Navarretia androsacea are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and injuries. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and it can also be dried and used as a poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
